content:
Overview

Cisco Networking Academy CCNA-track coursework, four courses, 2018–2019, high-school program. Hybrid lab environment — physical rack-mounted Cisco gear at school (2960 switches, 1941/2901 routers), Packet Tracer at home for between-class exercises. I sat the CCNA exam and didn't pass; the academy coursework was completed.

What the Coursework Covered

The standard four-semester CCNA-track sequence: networking fundamentals, routing and switching, scaling networks, connecting networks. Hands-on work was VLAN configuration, OSPF setup, port security, VLSM subnetting math, and OSI-layer troubleshooting in both Packet Tracer and on real gear. Studied work was the protocol theory underlying it — the parts of the exam I sat for.

What the Foundation Carries Forward

Networking literacy, mostly. I can read a packet capture and recognize what's on the wire. VLSM math transfers directly to cloud VPC CIDR planning — sizing subnets for environments is the same exercise. OSI-layer bottom-up debugging is still the discipline I apply to distributed systems failures: the leak is usually two layers below where the error message points. And reading Terraform networking modules — google_compute_subnetwork, aws_vpc, firewall rules — with comprehension instead of copy-pasting. Foundational coursework, not Cisco-engineer credentials. The resume credit is what it is.
